problems.title: Nonetype
problems.description: Write a Python function to nonetype and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- NoneType ---')

    print('Represents the absence of a value, similar to null in other languages.')

    a = None

    print('Example: a =', a)

    print('Type of a:', type(a))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: Numbers
problems.description: Write a Python function to numbers and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Numbers ---')

    print('Can be integers, floating-point numbers, or complex numbers.')

    a = 5

    print('\nInteger Example: a =', a)

    print('Type of a:', type(a))

    num = 2.5

    print('\nFloat Example: num =', num)

    print('Type of num:', type(num))

    num = 2 + 9j

    print('\nComplex Example: num =', num)

    print('Type of num:', type(num))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: Type Conversion
problems.description: Write a Python function to type conversion and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Type Conversion ---')

    print('Converting one data type to another.')

    a = 5.6

    b = int(a)

    k = float(b)

    c = complex(4, 5)

    print('Converted int:', b, 'Type:', type(b))

    print('Converted float:', k, 'Type:', type(k))

    print('Converted complex:', c, 'Type:', type(c))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: Booleans
problems.description: Write a Python function to booleans and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Booleans ---')

    print('Represent True or False.')

    a = True

    bool_val = 3 < 5

    print('Boolean Example: a =', a, 'Type:', type(a))

    print('Boolean Expression: 3 < 5 is', bool_val, 'Type:', type(bool_val))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: List
problems.description: Write a Python function to list and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- List ---')

    print('Ordered and mutable sequence of objects enclosed in square brackets.')

    lst = [25, 36, 45, 12]

    print('Example: lst =', lst)

    print('Type of lst:', type(lst))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: Tuple
problems.description: Write a Python function to tuple and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Tuple ---')

    print('Ordered and immutable sequence of objects enclosed in parentheses.')

    t = (25, 36, 45, 12, 7)

    print('Example: t =', t)

    print('Type of t:', type(t))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

problems.title: String
problems.description: Write a Python function to string and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- String ---')

    print('Sequence of characters enclosed in single or double quotes.')

    str_val = 'hello'

    print('Example: str_val =', str_val)

problems.title: Range
problems.description: Write a Python function to range and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Range ---')

    print('Immutable and iterable sequence of numbers.')

    r = range(10)

    print('Example: r =', r)

    print('Type of r:', type(r))

    print('List converted from range:', list(range(2, 10, 2)))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.

problems.title: Dictionary
problems.description: Write a Python function to dictionary and print the result.
problems.solution:
def main():
    print('\n--- Dictionary ---')

    print('Collection of key-value pairs enclosed in curly braces.')

    d = {1: 'a', 2: 'b', 3: 'c'}

    print('Example: d =', d)

    print('Type of d:', type(d))

    d1 = {'navin': 'samsung', 'rahul': 'iphone', 'kiran': 'oneplus'}

    print('Example: d1 =', d1)

    print('Values of d1:', d1.values())

    print('Keys of d1:', d1.keys())

    print("Accessing element by key 'rahul':", d1['rahul'])

    print("Using get method for key 'kiran':", d1.get('kiran'))

    print('\n' + '-' * 50 + '\n')

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
notes: This section is stored as a standalone reference snippet.
